inputs.label: Machine Purchase Cost
inputs.businessContext: Initial capital investment for the machine. Based on standard cost accounting principles (Maynard).

inputs.label: Useful Life (Years)
inputs.businessContext: Expected operational life of the machine. Typical range 5-20 years per industry standards (ISO 15663).

inputs.label: Salvage Value
inputs.businessContext: Estimated resale value at end of useful life. Usually 5-20% of purchase cost.

inputs.label: Annual Operating Hours
inputs.businessContext: Total hours the machine is operated per year. Based on typical single shift (2000 hrs) or multi-shift operations.

inputs.label: Annual Maintenance Cost
inputs.businessContext: Average yearly maintenance expenses including parts and labor. Typically 2-5% of purchase cost.

inputs.label: Energy Consumption (kW)
inputs.businessContext: Average power draw during operation. Based on machine specifications (ISO 50001).

inputs.label: Energy Cost per kWh
inputs.businessContext: Local electricity rate. Varies by region; typical range $0.05-$0.30.

inputs.label: Operator Labor Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage including benefits for machine operator. Based on labor market data.

inputs.label: Floor Space Occupied (sq ft)
inputs.businessContext: Area occupied by the machine including clearance. Used for space cost allocation.

inputs.label: Annual Facility Cost per sq ft
inputs.businessContext: Yearly cost per square foot for rent, utilities, insurance, etc. Typical $5-$20.

inputs.label: Other Annual Costs
inputs.businessContext: Miscellaneous costs such as consumables, software licenses, etc.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: User's confidence in input data accuracy. Affects adjustment factor for final rate.

thresholds.maintenanceCostPerYear > 0.05 * machineP: Maintenance cost exceeds 5% of purchase cost; consider preventive maintenance review.
thresholds.annualOperatingHours < 1000: Low utilization; consider alternative use or sell machine.
thresholds.energyConsumptionKw > 100: High energy consumption; consider energy efficiency audit.

formulas.totalAnnualCost: annualDepreciation + maintenanceCostPerYear + annualEnergyCost + (operatorLaborCostPerHour * annualOperatingHours) + annualFacilityCost + otherAnnualCosts

suggestedActions: If maintenance cost high: implement predictive maintenance program.
If low utilization: explore additional shifts or lease out machine.
If high energy consumption: retrofit with energy-efficient components.
